codeReview.summary: Excellent refactoring that replaces all magic numbers in main.go with well-named constants from the constants/ package. The implementation is clean, follows Go naming conventions (PascalCase for exported constants), and maintains full functional parity with the original code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the CLAUDE.md import ordering pattern (stdlib → third-party → internal). The constants package import was properly placed in the third-party group in main.go.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: main.go imports: github.com/divkix/Alita_Robot/alita/utils/constants is correctly placed between third-party and internal imports

sharedStateObservations.observation: All new constants follow the PascalCase naming convention for exported values, with clear semantic names that explain their purpose.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: alita/utils/constants/time.go: VeryLongTimeout, DefaultHTTPPort, MaxIdleConnsExtraBuffer, PreWarmConnectionAttempts, ShortDelay, ConnectionFastThreshold

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker properly categorized new constants by grouping them logically (Connection pooling, Retry and delay durations) which enhances maintainability.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: time.go: Constants organized with comments like '// Connection pooling' and '// Retry and delay durations'

summary: The fix-magic-numbers feature successfully replaces 9 magic number occurrences in main.go with named constants. All 22 test packages passed, build succeeded, and lint passed. The constants are well-named, properly categorized, and follow Go naming conventions. No blocking issues found - this is a clean refactoring that improves code maintainability.
